classes.name: icu.keyhandler.KeyHandler

methods.name: icu.keyhandler.KeyHandler.press
documentation:
Called when a keyboard key is pressed.
Args:
    event (keyevent): A key event, should contain the attribute keysym (a unique identifier for the key).

methods.name: icu.keyhandler.KeyHandler.release
documentation:
Called when a keyboard key is released.
Args:
    event (keyevent): A key event, should contain the attribute keysym (a unique identifier for the key).

methods.name: icu.keyhandler.KeyHandler.isPressed
documentation:
is the given key currently being pressed?

Args:
    key ( -- ): the unique identifier associated with the key (see press, release) 

Returns:
    bool: True if the key is currently pressed, False otherwise.

methods.name: icu.keyhandler.KeyHandler.hold
documentation:
periodically trigger key events to the given component

Args:
    component (str): sink name to receive key events
    key (str): key that is to be sent
    data (str): any additional event data to be sent...

Returns:
    KeyHoldGenerator: event generator.
A KeyHandler records keyboard key press and release events.
TODO define commonly used key codes  (Left, Right, Up, Down, A, S, W, D) + Joystick??
consider using os.system('xset r off') # debouncing...

classes.name: icu.keyhandler.KeyHoldGenerator
documentation: Periodically generally key events when a key is being held down.

modules.name: icu.overlay

classes.name: icu.overlay.Overlay
documentation: A GUI widget that is placed above other widgets. Accepts 'move' and 'place' events to move the widget.

modules.name: icu
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: icu.get_event_sources
documentation: TODO others?

functions.name: icu.start
documentation:
Start ICU as a seperate process. 

Args:
    sinks (list, optional): [description]. Defaults to [].
    sources (list, optional): [description]. Defaults to [].

functions.name: icu.run
documentation:
Starts the ICU system. Call blocks until the GUI is closed.

Args:
    shared: shared memory - one end of a pipe that can receive data, used to expose various useful attributes in ICU.
    sinks (list, optional): A list of external sinks, used to receive events from the ICU system. Defaults to [].
    sources (list, optional): A list of external sources, used to send events to the ICU system. Defaults to [].
    config (str): Path of configuration file.
global config
config = None

functions.name: icu.task_system_monitor
documentation:
Set up system monitoring task event schedules

Args:
    config (SimpleNamespace): configuration options

functions.name: icu.task_tracking
documentation:
Set up tracking task event schedules

Args:
    config (SimpleNamespace): configuration options

functions.name: icu.task_fuel_monitor
documentation:
Set up fuel monitoring task event scheduless

Args:
    config (SimpleNamespace): configuration options

class_variables.name: icu.fuel_monitor.Pump.__components__

modules.name: icu.eyetracking

functions.name: icu.eyetracking.eyetracker
documentation:
Creates a new Eyetracker (there should only ever be one).
Args:
    root (tk): tk root window.
    filter: a filter for x,y - averaging, gaze points etc (see eyetracker.filter)
    sample_rate (int, optional): number of samples (events) per second. Defaults to 300.
    calibrate (bool, optional): calibrate the eyetracker. Defaults to True.
    stub (bool, optional): use a stub class (see StubEyeTracker) if hardware is not available. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    (EyeTracker): The new EyeTracker.

classes.name: icu.eyetracking.EyeTrackingError
documentation: An error that may be thrown by an EyeTracker, typically will wrap a psychopy error.

classes.name: icu.eyetracking.EyeTrackerStub

methods.name: icu.eyetracking.EyeTrackerStub.update
documentation:
Called when the mouse moves, updates internal mouse (x,y).

Args:
    event (mouseevent): a mouse event generated that defines x,y attributes.

methods.name: icu.eyetracking.EyeTrackerStub.run
documentation: Generates events that move the current overlay (if it exists).

methods.name: icu.eyetracking.EyeTrackerStub.close
documentation: Force the thread to exit.

A stub EyeTracker class that uses the current mouse position as a stand in for gaze position, 
use for testing without eyetracking hardware.

functions.name: icu.event.add_event_source
documentation:
Add an external event source to ICU. Any events generated by 
this source will be propagated to an ICU event sink.

functions.name: icu.event.add_event_sink
documentation:
Add an external event sink to ICU. This event sink will receive 
all events that are generated by the ICU system.

classes.name: icu.event.ExternalEventSource

methods.name: icu.event.ExternalEventSource.source
documentation:
Send a new event to the ICU system.

Args:
    src (str): the name of the source object (a unique ID)
    dst (str): the name of the destination (sink) object (a unique ID), see get_event_sources() for a list of source IDs.
    timestamp (float, optional): floating point number expressed in seconds since the epoch, in UTC (see time.time()). Defaults to the current time (on event instantiation).

A thread-safe event source to be used externally as a
mechanism for sending events to the ICU system.

classes.name: icu.event.ExternalEventSink

methods.name: icu.event.ExternalEventSink.get
documentation: Pop from event buffer.

A thread-safe event sink to be used externally as a 
mechanism for receiving events from the ICU system.

classes.name: icu.event.EventCallback
documentation: Used internally by widgets that can receive and generate events.

functions.name: icu.config.save
documentation:
Save config to a file.

Args:

functions.name: icu.config.load

Args:

Returns:

Args:
